consequence.description: Using float64 unnecessarily doubles memory consumption, causing out-of-memory errors on large historical datasets; using float16 causes accumulated rounding errors in profit calculations, distorting strategy performance metrics

evidence_refs.summary: 3a76235 Derived from BD: BD-002

core.action: Assume the framework handles currency precision correctly using float types — the framework lacks explicit Decimal handling for monetary values, causing potential floating-point rounding errors in currency calculations

consequence.description: Using float for currency operations introduces binary floating-point representation errors (e.g., 0.1 + 0.2 != 0.3), causing silent discrepancies in profit calculations and account balance tracking that accumulate over many transactions

evidence_refs.summary: 3a76235 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-019

core.when: When implementing any currency or monetary value handling in the trading system
core.action: Use Python Decimal type with explicit precision context for each currency calculations — convert float price data to Decimal before monetary arithmetic, and format output as string to avoid float representation issues

consequence.description: Without explicit Decimal handling, currency calculations suffer from float rounding errors that cause account balance discrepancies, incorrect P&L reporting, and potential margin calculation failures in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: 3a76235 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-019

evidence_refs.summary: 3a76235 Derived from BD: BD-013
